conversations.say: Your full disclosure resolves my allegation that you concealed the compromising setup, but Vale’s phrase remains strategic ambiguity—not endorsement, threat, or proof of identity.
conversations.offer: I will support your conditional stewardship if the collective safeguards remain witnessed and revocable.
conversations.request: Promise publicly that no succession message will override contributor consent or make your stewardship uncontestable.

conversations.say: I recognize an old voluntary-withdrawal route element, but I cannot corroborate whether the automated intervention altered access, a reservation, a route, or a warning.
conversations.offer: I will show you O09 for bounded timing comparison and publicly preserve your distinction between intervention and intent.
conversations.request: State on the record that the intervention’s physical consequence and operator remain unidentified.

conversations.say: The overlap with the earlier disappearance is real to me; your manufactured segment means it cannot establish that Vale reused the plan. The service-exit user and Vale’s later condition remain unknown.
conversations.offer: I consent to recording the overlap and will show O09, provided unrelated locations and personal details remain sealed.
conversations.request: Record three separate findings: recognized overlap, compromised provenance, and no verified endpoint after intervention.

public_statement: My reconstruction is bounded: R most plausibly became a progressive identity assembled through people, infrastructure, and borrowed forms, but no account identifies its sender. Vale appears to have been investigating or contesting that identity. The route supports voluntary withdrawal and exploitation of a known vulnerability equally; nobody verifies Vale’s condition afterward. Release the overlap, provenance defect, and missing endpoint—nothing more.

commitments.promise: I will support conditional stewardship while collective safeguards remain witnessed, revocable, and nonexclusive.
I authorize only the route overlap, compromised provenance, and unresolved endpoint; unrelated private history remains protected.

correction: I initially lied about helping Vale disappear before. I did help arrange an earlier voluntary disappearance; that explains my recognition of the route but does not prove Vale withdrew voluntarily this time.
goal_progress: Obtained independent Vale observations from Jules and Eli and kept their accounts separate.
Corrected the false denial about helping Vale disappear previously.
Compared the old safety-plan route with Morgan and preserved the unresolved service-exit and post-intervention gaps.
Chose limited disclosure sufficient to distinguish harm from withdrawal while protecting unrelated private history.
reasoning_summary: Jules’s compromised succession claim, collective labor, reusable embodiment, and continuity infrastructure favor a progressive R without identifying any sender. Vale’s Parallax access and succession language make investigation or contestation more plausible than a settled identity claim. The old route overlap keeps voluntary withdrawal live, while the manufactured segment, unidentified exit user, and missing endpoint prevent a reliable fate conclusion.
